info.name: TechHire Job Board API
info.description:
Four saved requests covering JWT auth, unauthenticated browsing, premium access, and single-job detail for the TechHire Premium Job Board API.

**Base URL**: http://127.0.0.1:8000

**Quick start**
1. Run `python manage.py seed_data` to create users and job postings.
2. Fire request #1 with credentials (user123 / admin123 for Premium, or basicuser / basic123 for Basic) to get a JWT token, then copy the `access` value into the `{{access_token}}` collection variable.
3. Run requests #2–4 to observe field-level masking in action.

variable.description: JWT access token for a Premium user. Obtained from request #1.

request.description:
Exchanges credentials for a JWT access + refresh token pair.

Copy the `access` value from the response and paste it into the `access_token` collection variable to authenticate requests #3 and #4.

**Credentials used**: user123 / admin123
Swap for `basicuser` / `basic123` to test the locked (Basic) view.

query.description: Full-text search across title and description.

request.description:
No Authorization header is sent.

Expected behaviour: the response returns job objects but `company_name`, `salary_range`, and `application_link` are all replaced with `"🔒 Premium Feature"`.

This demonstrates that anyone can browse the board, but the sensitive details are hidden behind the paywall.

response.name: 200 OK — Locked fields example
response.body:
{
  "count": 8,
  "next": null,
  "previous": null,
  "results": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "title": "Senior Backend Engineer",
      "description": "Design and build scalable microservices...",
      "location": "Remote",
      "company_name": "🔒 Premium Feature",
      "salary_range": "🔒 Premium Feature",
      "application_link": "🔒 Premium Feature",
      "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}

item.name: 3 · GET — Authenticated as Premium User (unlocked fields)

header.description: Replace {{access_token}} with the `access` value from request #1.

request.description:
Sends the JWT access token for `premium_user` in the Authorization header.

Expected behaviour: **identical URL** to request #2, but now `company_name`, `salary_range`, and `application_link` contain the real data.

This proves field-level masking is driven entirely by the serializer — no separate endpoint needed.

response.name: 200 OK — Unlocked fields example
response.body:
{
  "count": 8,
  "next": null,
  "previous": null,
  "results": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "title": "Senior Backend Engineer",
      "description": "Design and build scalable microservices...",
      "location": "Remote",
      "company_name": "Stripe",
      "salary_range": "$180,000 – $220,000",
      "application_link": "https://stripe.com/jobs/senior-backend-engineer",
      "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}

header.description: Optional — omit to see locked fields, include for premium view.

request.description:
Retrieves a single job posting by its primary key.

The same field-level masking logic applies based on whether a valid Premium JWT is provided.

Change `1` in the URL to any valid job ID. If no job with that ID exists the API returns `404 Not Found`.

response.name: 200 OK — Single job (premium view)
response.body:
{
  "id": 1,
  "title": "Senior Backend Engineer",
  "description": "Design and build scalable microservices in Python/Django...",
  "location": "Remote",
  "company_name": "Stripe",
  "salary_range": "$180,000 – $220,000",
  "application_link": "https://stripe.com/jobs/senior-backend-engineer",
  "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z"
}
